verse_specific_anchors: mahā-rathāḥ — great chariot-warriors / competent peers; the specific witness-class restriction
bhayāt — out of fear; the peer-default-cognition framing
bahu-mataḥ — highly esteemed; the prior-standing in the network
lāghavam — lightness; the becoming-small phase change in network model
verdict_reason: The engineering analog anchors specifically on mahā-rathāḥ as competent-peer-class restriction. The Kubernetes-operator + sabbatical-mid-cutover scenario is uniquely 2.35's analog: it requires the technically-competent-peer-witness frame, not the broader bhūtāni frame of 2.34. The bahu-mataḥ → lāghavam phase change is irreducibly 2.35's: 2.34 names imperishable disrepute (broad), 2.35 names the specific peer-network model-update from highly-esteemed to small. Other karma-yoga verses cannot be force-fit: 2.31 names the role-bound source of duty; 2.32 names unsought-arrival; 2.33 names if-then conditional loss; 2.34 names imperishable disrepute. Only 2.35 names the competent-peer-witness model-update structurally. PASS on both 7.6 and 3.5.
